topology_note: 1:N roster extension on an existing stack-topology node. galar-gym-challenge gains its 10th endpoint (League Staff, the cohort's first labor-rank-and-file member alongside the existing Gym-Leader-celebrity-athlete tier and the Champion tier). The stack widens at a new institutional tier — the node's three-tier structure (rank-and-file staff → Gym Leaders → Champion) is now fully populated across 3 of 3 institutional tiers plus 2 place-cards.
category_note: Institution-level identity wiring. League Staff is canonically a Galar Pokemon League Trainer class per Bulbapedia, and the card's oracle text explicitly references Wyndon Stadium ('If Wyndon Stadium is in play, draw 2 more cards') — the mechanical interaction with the venue card from the same set is the designer-coordinated marker of the within-set institutional micro-cohort (VIV-153 staff + VIV-161 venue). Hero's Medal (VIV-152) was considered and rejected per option B from the hot-context prompt: it depicts a TCG-mechanical item with no named institutional Bulbapedia lore (the Bulbapedia Hero's Medal page is a TCG-only entry with no in-game-lore content), making it a tag-bridge anchor at best rather than an institutional-identity anchor.

endpoints.name: League Staff (Vivid Voltage 153/185)
endpoints.role: new corpus member joining the stack — first labor-rank-and-file endpoint
endpoints.card_subtype: Trainer Supporter card depicting two uniformed Galar Pokemon League staff workers in black-and-red livery

description: Galar Gym Challenge node extension. League Staff (Vivid Voltage 153/185) depicts the league's salaried labor tier — two uniformed staff workers in matching black-and-red livery standing in an indoor arena corridor, the cohort's rank-and-file workforce staffing the Champion Cup tournament infrastructure. The card's oracle text explicitly references Wyndon Stadium ('If Wyndon Stadium is in play, draw 2 more cards'), mechanically encoding the staff's working-relationship to the venue and confirming designer-coordinated pairing with the wave 33 E1 Wyndon Stadium card (VIV-161 + VIV-153 at adjacent collector numbers within the same set, mechanical interaction text between them — the Vivid Voltage venue-and-staff designer-paired micro-cohort, parallel to the Darkness Ablaze Rose + Rose Tower chairman-and-HQ pair at adjacent collector numbers DAA-168 + DAA-169 within the macro-cosmos cohort). This is the node's first labor-rank-and-file endpoint vs the existing Gym-Leader-celebrity-athlete endpoints; the apparatus-of-spectacle thesis deepens at the salaried-worker tier. The league cohort now spans three institutional tiers: rank-and-file staff (League Staff) → Gym Leader-celebrity-athletes (Allister/Bea/Kabu/Melony/Milo/Nessa/Opal) → Champion (Leon), with two place-cards (Turffield Stadium, Wyndon Stadium) anchoring the geography from opening-Gym-town to closing-Champion-Cup-arena.

[Corpus: cards/pokemon/vivid-voltage/161-185-wyndon-stadium.md — adjacent collector number within Vivid Voltage; League Staff's oracle text explicitly references this card by name ('If Wyndon Stadium is in play, draw 2 more cards')]

cards/_characters/galar-gym-challenge.md — extended `appears_on:` list with `pokemon/vivid-voltage/153-185-league-staff`; added League Staff body bullet to the 'Cards in our corpus pointing here (current)' section with three-tier-cohort callout (rank-and-file staff / Gym Leader / Champion)

decision_rationale_options_considered.option_B_chosen: Add League Staff to galar-gym-challenge only; skip Hero's Medal. League Staff is canonically a Galar Pokemon League Trainer class with documented in-game role per Bulbapedia; it's clearly an institutional-identity anchor. Hero's Medal is a TCG-mechanical Pokemon Tool item with no named in-game institutional lore — Bulbapedia's Hero's Medal page is a TCG-only entry (no story content); making it a card with single corpus appearance and no institutional cohort connection beyond 'Galar set'. Tag-bridge at best.
decision_rationale_options_considered.option_A_rejected: Add both to galar-gym-challenge as institutional-ecosystem extensions. Rejected because Hero's Medal lacks the named-institutional-lore anchor that League Staff has — it'd be a thin tag-bridge wiring rather than a load-bearing edge per Edgelord's standards.
decision_rationale_options_considered.option_C_rejected: Hero's Medal → potential cards/_symbols/galar-championship-medal.md future symbol node. Rejected because: (a) only 1 corpus card depicts the item (premature for a symbol node per the orzhov-signet 3-card precedent); (b) Hero's Medal is a TCG-mechanical Pokemon Tool item with no in-game canonical award status (Bulbapedia confirms no story content for the medal); (c) symbol nodes anchor iconographic primitives with named designer-confirmed canonical content (the orzhov-signet eclipsed-sun's master's-medallion / slave-brand canonical lore per Magic flavor text). Hero's Medal lacks any equivalent named iconographic anchor.

claims_made_against_canonical_sources: League Staff is a Galar Pokemon League Trainer class — Bulbapedia: League Staff (Trainer class) (primary canon)
The card depicts two staff workers in the canonical black-and-red Galar League livery — vision pass confirmation
The card's oracle text references Wyndon Stadium — PokemonTCG.io swsh4-153 (primary source)
Wyndon Stadium and League Staff are at adjacent collector numbers (161 and 153) within Vivid Voltage — set-internal printing-side fact
claims_held_back_per_anti_confab_discipl: Did NOT name the two depicted figures as specific characters. They are uniformed staff workers — anonymous-class depictions, not specific named NPCs. The vision pass correctly identifies them as 'two uniformed League Staff workers' without claiming individual identity.

Did NOT extend the node to claim 'rank-and-file labor cohort' as a permanent three-tier-cohort subtype on the node body. The body bullet for League Staff uses 'first labor-rank-and-file endpoint' as a descriptor, not as a formal sub-cohort declaration; if more rank-and-file Galar Pokemon League cards land (League Staff Full Art variant, future Trainer-class Supporter cards depicting the labor tier), the node body's three-tier declaration may warrant promotion to a formal Caveat section. Single-instance descriptor for now.
Did NOT propose a Hero's Medal symbol node. Surfaced as a future-flag with explicit reason: insufficient corpus support + no named iconographic Bulbapedia lore.
Did NOT touch tags_hub or tags_filter on the League Staff card. Tag-layer work is bbl-researcher's domain.
Did NOT modify the Vision section of the League Staff card. Vision belongs to bbl-researcher.

Did NOT cross-mirror the connection back to the Wyndon Stadium card's Connections section (that secondary mirror was already authored in E1's Wyndon Stadium Connections section as the staff-and-venue pair — the secondary card-to-card chain is anchored from the venue side; League Staff card's Connections section anchors the equivalent from the staff side. Bidirectional mirror is achieved across the two slots' work product without double-writing.)
anti_confab_audit.no_extrapolation_beyond_cited_sources: PASSED. Every claim in the proposed body bullet and Connections section traces to either (a) the League Staff card's own vision pass + oracle text, (b) the Wyndon Stadium card's own oracle text, (c) Bulbapedia's Galar League / League Staff canonical pages, or (d) the galar-gym-challenge node's already-canonicalized body content.

mood_justification: Clean 1:N roster extension with a designer-coordinated micro-cohort underlay. The primary edge (League Staff → galar-gym-challenge node) is a textbook institutional-identity anchor with Bulbapedia primary-source backing. The secondary card-to-card pairing surfaced (League Staff oracle text referencing Wyndon Stadium by name, both at adjacent collector numbers within Vivid Voltage) is a Wizards-of-Pokemon-coordinated set-internal micro-cohort that strengthens the wiring — the kind of within-set designer-pairing that the bbl-bundler will treat as a cohesion signal. Refusing the Hero's Medal candidate per option B was the correct restraint: tag-bridge anchors don't earn edges. Edgelord-with-CBG-energy: worst proposed dual-wiring ever rejected (Hero's Medal as a Galar-cohort anchor on the basis of being a 'medal in a Galar set'? no Bulbapedia lore page? a TCG-only Pokemon Tool with no in-game canon? get out. catalogue failure mode: tag-bridge-disguised-as-institutional-anchor). The League Staff edge stands on its own canonical merit. Worth the work.
